reforms.description: In 1998, the Czech government introduced a comprehensive set of investment incentives aimed at attracting foreign direct investment and enhancing innovation. This included the abolition of tax holidays in favor of activity-specific allowances and credits for expenditure on new plant and machinery, as well as relief from customs duties and subsidies for employee education. The programme was further enhanced in 1999 to lower the threshold for support and increase job-creation grants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to stimulate innovation and investment, thereby enhancing economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of investment incentives, aimed at fostering innovation and attracting foreign investment.
reforms.source_quote: In 1998, the authorities introduced a range of incentives including relief from customs duties for certain kinds of investment goods.
